Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile exhaustive async states, immutable stages, atomic commit, post-commit effects, and counts; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Pass immutable stage outputs and commit only one admitted unit rather than mutating a shared feed object across awaits.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts No Shared Mutation model · Theory Feed Ts No Sha…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Single-threaded JavaScript prevents async state races.
